comment: Quality Gates catalog — structure dimension. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: quality/gates/structure/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/structure/.

the historical milestones index paragraph (one paragraph at the top with relative links to .planning/milestones/v0.X.0-phases/{REQUIREMENTS,ROADMAP}.md) is permitted in both files

asserts: P57 stub: row exists with verifier.script pointing at quality/gates/docs-build/badges-resolve.py (P60 ships the verifier per BADGE-01 traceability); the row anchors the contract from P57 onward; status: NOT-VERIFIED is acceptable until P60 ships the verifier.

rows.owner_hint: P60 docs-build dimension ships the verifier; for P57 the row's existence is the contract

asserts: bash quality/gates/structure/banned-words.sh exits 0 (no banned-word violations in docs/)

rows.owner_hint: any docs/ file using banned terms (FUSE, fusermount, kernel, partial-clone, etc. — see docs/.banned-words.toml for the full matrix)

asserts: no committed file on the outgoing ref-range matches the credential-prefix regex set: ATATT3[A-Za-z0-9_+/=-]{20,} (Atlassian); Bearer ATATT3 ; ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,} (GitHub classic PAT); github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{20,} (GitHub fine-grained PAT); AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35} (Google API key, added after a secret-scanning alert that the owner triaged as a false positive but which exposed a real pattern-coverage gap); AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16} (AWS access key id); xox[baprs]-[0-9A-Za-z-]{10,} (Slack token); sk-(proj-)?[A-Za-z0-9]{20,} (OpenAI API key -- alnum-run form, tightened from sk-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,} to avoid kebab-case doc-slug collisions); [-]----BEGIN( RSA| EC| OPENSSH)? PRIVATE KEY----- (PEM private-key header; leading [-] char class avoids grep parsing the pattern as an option) -- the four AWS/Slack/OpenAI/PEM patterns added 2026-07-04 (D-CONV-4) as the zero-dep local layer beside the gitleaks CI backstop

the verifier respects the same EXCLUDE_DIRS as the existing hook (.git, target, node_modules, .githooks, quality/gates/structure -- the last is a load-bearing self-exclusion so the gate doesn't flag its own pattern literals)
the verifier exits 1 on any hit so pre-push BLOCKS the push (matches existing hook semantics; non-negotiable per CLAUDE.md threat model)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a real token shape was committed -- ROTATE THE TOKEN at the issuing platform BEFORE doing anything else (Atlassian: https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens; GitHub: https://github.com/settings/tokens; Google: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials)

asserts: find .planning -maxdepth 1 -type f \( -name '*MILESTONE-AUDIT*.md' -o -name 'SESSION-END-STATE*' \) | grep -v archive returns empty stdout

rows.owner_hint: any new milestone-audit or session-end-state document at .planning/ top level -- relocate under .planning/milestones/audits/ or .planning/archive/

asserts: For each docs/*.md whose byte size <500 AND whose top-level slug appears as a key in mkdocs.yml plugins.redirects.redirect_maps, the redirect target resolves to a file present under docs/. Stubs without a redirect entry AND without a nav: entry are flagged.

rows.owner_hint: any new top-level docs/<slug>.md stub <500 bytes -- add to mkdocs.yml redirect_maps or remove

asserts: File quality/reports/audits/repo-org-gaps.md exists

File contains a top-section with audit table mapping every numbered gap from .planning/research/v0.11.1/repo-organization-gaps.md 'Top 10 cleanup recommendations' to a closure path (closed-by-catalog-row | closed-by-existing-gate | waived)

rows.owner_hint: Wave 2 produces the audit; future repo-org audits land at the same path

asserts: File quality/catalogs/doc-alignment.json exists and json.loads parses without error
Top-level keys schema_version, summary, rows are all present
schema_version equals '1.0'

rows.owner_hint: if RED: quality/catalogs/doc-alignment.json missing or unparseable -- restore from P64 Wave 1 commit OR recover via /reposix-quality-backfill

asserts: summary block has all 9 required keys: claims_total, claims_bound, claims_missing_test, claims_retire_proposed, claims_retired, alignment_ratio, floor, trend_30d, last_walked
alignment_ratio equals claims_bound / max(1, claims_total - claims_retired) within 0.001 epsilon (when claims_total == 0, ratio is 1.0 by definition)
floor is a number in [0.0, 1.0]

rows.owner_hint: if RED: summary block hand-edited or runner mutation diverged from row counts -- run /reposix-quality-refresh quality/catalogs/doc-alignment.json (or fix manually)

sources: .planning/research/v0.12.0-docs-alignment-design/02-architecture.md:Floor only ratchets up via deliberate human commit

asserts: git log -p --follow -- quality/catalogs/doc-alignment.json shows the floor field never decreased between commits (monotone non-decreasing)
floor regressions are an audit signal of an accidental ratchet-down; the commit SHA is named in the artifact for owner triage

rows.owner_hint: if RED: an earlier commit ratcheted the floor DOWN (audit signal) -- ratchet floor only via deliberate human commit, never auto

asserts: target/release/reposix-quality (or target/debug/reposix-quality fallback) exists and is executable
reposix-quality walk exits 0 when every row's source_hash + test_body_hash match the on-disk content
reposix-quality walk exits non-zero on any blocking row state (STALE_DOCS_DRIFT, MISSING_TEST, STALE_TEST_GONE, TEST_MISALIGNED, RETIRE_PROPOSED) AND prints the slash-command hint to stderr verbatim
the walker NEVER refreshes stored hashes; only `bind` does, and only after grader returns GREEN (asserted by crates/reposix-quality/tests/walk.rs)
on the empty-state catalog (P64 Wave 1; rows == []), the walker exits 0

GRADE/PERSIST split (walk.sh): a bare grade run (no --persist) walks a throwaway copy and leaves quality/catalogs/doc-alignment.json byte-identical, so `git status` stays clean; only `walk.sh --persist` writes the committed catalog -- run.py never forwards --persist to a verifier, so pre-push/pre-pr GATE runs are grade-only

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs-alignment hash drift detected -- run /reposix-quality-refresh <doc> from a fresh top-level Claude session (cannot run from inside /gsd-execute-phase). Stderr names the affected doc.

asserts: release-plz.toml exists at repo root
release-plz.toml contains a [workspace] table
release-plz.toml contains 'git_release_enable = false' (per-package GitHub release creation disabled so the canonical v* release stays releases/latest)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: someone deleted release-plz.toml or flipped git_release_enable. The fix is preventive — without it, release-plz creates one zero-asset GH release per workspace package on every push to main, stealing releases/latest from the canonical v* release. See PR #34 (commit f1d89e5) for the original rationale.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If per-package GitHub releases were re-enabled (release-plz.toml deleted, or git_release_enable flipped away from false), the verifier's grep for the exact file/table/key would come back empty or find the wrong value, exiting non-zero and flipping the row to FAIL -- directly falsifying the claim that per-package releases stay disabled and releases/latest stays pointed at the canonical v* tag.

asserts: every tracked file is within its extension's progressive-disclosure budget (CLAUDE.md=40k, .claude/skills/=10k, *.md=20k, *.rs=20k, *.py=15k, *.sh=10k); auto-generated + crates source excluded
Cargo.lock, quality/catalogs/*.json, quality/reports/, crates/.../tests/fixtures/, CHANGELOG.md, and crates/.../*.rs are excluded from scope

OVER-BUDGET tier (pct>=100, size>limit): verifier exit 1 on any violation by default; the --warn-only flag flips ONLY this tier's exit to 0 (transitional cleanup window). This tier's block-later contract reactivates when --warn-only/the waiver are removed on 2026-08-08.

EARLY-WARNING tier (75<=pct<100, pct=size*100/limit integer): files APPROACHING but not yet over the ceiling emit a non-blocking WARN summary to stderr — one header line naming the count + 'consider a progressive-disclosure split', then band files sorted by pct DESC (top-12, then '… and N more at ≥75%' when >12), each 'file — size/limit chars (PCT%)'. This WARN is print-only (no WARN verdict status in verdict.py; precedent: the pre-push timing tripwire), ALWAYS emitted independent of --warn-only/the waiver, and NEVER affects the exit code in either flag mode. Self-test: quality/gates/structure/file-size-limits.selftest.sh.

rows.owner_hint: if violations: split the file per the hint emitted by the verifier (CLAUDE.md=40k, .claude/skills/=10k, *.md=20k, *.rs=20k, *.py=15k, *.sh=10k). Auto-generated/lockfile/catalog/fixture/CHANGELOG paths are excluded; *.rs in crates/ is also excluded pending source-code-budget cleanup in a future milestone.

waiver.reason: Re-instates the original 'warn-now, block-later' contract from commit 8ff9a66 that introduced this gate. Verifier args carry --warn-only so the verifier exits 0 with a structured warning list; the catalog row stays visible (status FAIL surfaces the count) but pre-push does not block. NARROWED 2026-07-11 by P103 Lane B (OP-8 file-size drain): the two BIG offenders — .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/SURPRISES-INTAKE.md (109k) and GOOD-TO-HAVES.md (129k) — were split into per-part child dirs (surprises-intake/, good-to-haves/, byte-exact round-trip proven, INDEX rewritten in place) via the new reusable scripts/split_ledger.py, and are NO LONGER waived. Residual violations: 56 files (45 *.md over the 20k budget, 6 *.py over 15k, 5 *.sh over 10k) — RE-COUNTED 2026-07-11; full live list: bash quality/gates/structure/file-size-limits.sh (default/blocking) or --warn-only. Deferred set + per-file drain sketch is filed at .planning/milestones/v0.14.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md § GOOD-TO-HAVES-02 (file-size-drain-residual). Categories: [45 *.md] live planning ledgers (STATE.md — concurrent-writer, never hand-edit; PROJECT, CONSULT-DECISIONS, ORCHESTRATION, RETROSPECTIVE), milestone ROADMAP/REQUIREMENTS x3 milestones (monolithic-coupled to GSD tooling + the no-loose-roadmap invariant — defer until a shard convention exists), archived phase bundles (.planning/phases/89-91/*, 93-RELIEF-HANDOFF, 102-.../PLAN, 97-HANDOVER), research bundles (.planning/research/v0.13.0-real-backend-frictions/* + v0.13.2-cross-link-fidelity/*), docs (troubleshooting, reference/cli, decisions/010) + quality/PROTOCOL.md + .claude/skills/decision-procedures/SKILL.md. [6 *.py] quality/runners/{run, verdict, _audit_field, test_audit_field, test_realbackend}.py + scripts/shell_coverage.py (_audit_field.py + test_audit_field.py were under concurrent-lane edit during P103 — splitting is a module refactor with collision risk). [5 *.sh] .claude/hooks/leaf-isolation-guard.sh + .githooks/test-pre-push.sh + quality/gates/agent-ux/{dark-factory/dvcs-third-arm, real-git-push-e2e, zero-shot-onboarding}.sh (need a lib/ source-helper convention before factoring). Drain reuse: scripts/split_ledger.py handles any `## `-delimited ledger directly (sub-splits an over-budget single entry at `---` boundaries). Tracked as a +2 reservation candidate for P111 / the milestone after v0.14.0.
waiver.dimension_owner: structure dimension owner (currently maintained by orchestrator)
waiver.tracked_in: v0.14.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md § GOOD-TO-HAVES-02 (file-size-drain-residual; P103 Lane B split the two big v0.13.0 intakes 2026-07-11). Origin: v0.13.0 extension SURPRISES-INTAKE (file-size-limits warn-only re-instated 2026-05-08).

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If a tracked file blew past its extension's progressive-disclosure budget, the verifier's per-extension byte-count scan would report the specific path + size and (absent --warn-only) exit non-zero -- directly falsifying the claim that every tracked file fits its budget. The active waiver enumerates the 56 known residual violations (45 *.md / 6 *.py / 5 *.sh, re-counted 2026-07-11 after P103 Lane B split the two big v0.13.0 intakes out of scope); a NEW, unenumerated violation would still surface in the verifier's own warning output for the next waiver-renewal review to catch.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-04 (P89 89-02) — bans v0.13+ phase-ID tokens (regex \bP[0-9]{2,3}-[0-9]+\b) in production Rust source. Rust-native sibling of scripts/banned-words-lint.sh (docs-scoped); allowlist marker is `// banned-words: ok`. The regex intentionally misses v0.8/v0.9 audit IDs (P1-1 shape) — see the 89-02 script header for the trade-off. Minted NOT-VERIFIED in the P89 catalog-first commit (89-01); verifier ships in 89-02.

asserts: grep -nHE '\bP[0-9]{2,3}-[0-9]+\b' over crates/**/*.rs (excluding tests/, **/CHANGELOG.md, allowlist-marker lines) returns zero matches
violation lines emit owner_hint pointing at allowlist marker convention

rows.owner_hint: add '// banned-words: ok' allowlist marker OR rename the phase-id token
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier asserts `grep -nHE '\bP[0-9]{2,3}-[0-9]+\b' crates/**/*.rs` (excluding tests/ and allowlist-marker lines and **/CHANGELOG.md) returns zero matches. If the description's claim ('phase IDs are banned in production source') were false — e.g., a `P79-02 scaffold` literal stayed in `crates/reposix-cli/src/attach.rs` — the assertion would fail because grep would report the match line and the verifier would exit 1.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-05 (P89 89-05) — cross-references deferral pointers in crates/ ('not yet wired in P\d+' / 'lands? (alongside|in) P\d+' / 'substrate-gap-deferred') against the named phase's PLAN file existence; ALSO BLOCKs explicitly on bare deferral matches carrying NO phase number. Minted NOT-VERIFIED in the P89 catalog-first commit (89-01); verifier ships in 89-05.

asserts: every match of 'not yet wired in P\d+' / 'lands? (alongside|in) P\d+' / 'substrate-gap-deferred' in crates/ resolves to .planning/phases/N-*/PLAN*.md existing
missing PLAN file BLOCKs with structured stderr naming the file:line + missing phase number
deferral pattern match with NO phase number (PNN suffix) BLOCKs explicitly rather than silently passing

rows.owner_hint: create the named phase's PLAN file or scrub the deferral pointer or add the missing PNN suffix
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier asserts that for every match of three deferral patterns (`not yet wired in P\d+`, `lands? (alongside|in) P\d+`, `substrate-gap-deferred`) in `crates/`, the named phase number resolves to a `.planning/phases/N-*/` directory containing at least one `*PLAN*.md` file AND that any deferral-pattern match yields >=1 extracted phase number (else BLOCK explicitly with stderr citing the missing PNN suffix). If the description's claim ('deferral pointers cross-reference an existing downstream PLAN') were false — e.g., a comment said `// substrate-gap-deferred until P150 lands` and no .planning/phases/150-* exists, OR a comment said `// substrate-gap-deferred` with no phase number at all — the assertion would fail because the find command would return empty (orphan-PNN case) OR the PNN-extraction would yield zero matches (silent-pass case) and the verifier would exit 1 naming the bad reference.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-11 (P89 89-07; grandfather rule fixed P109 / D2-RBF-FW11-GRANDFATHER-FIX-01) — load-time runner cross-check: every catalog row minted on/after 2026-05-08 must carry a claim_vs_assertion_audit string >=50 chars stating how the verifier's asserts would falsify the row's description claim if false; kind:shell-subprocess rows must ALSO declare a transcript-emitting contract (transcript_path). GRANDFATHER: rows present at the RBF-FW-11-landing commit (8413184) are exempt even when their last_verified is null — catalog_dirty() drops last_verified from unchanged rows (run.py:502 rolls it back), so a legacy row routinely presents null; the grandfather set keys off LANDING-COMMIT PRESENCE (GRANDFATHERED_NULL_LV in _audit_field.py), NOT a null-last_verified proxy. A null-last_verified row NOT in that set is still forced to carry the field (closes the omit-last_verified dodge). Pre-cutoff legacy rows (P78-P88) validate without the field until P95 RBF-D-06 backfills. Minted NOT-VERIFIED in the P89 catalog-first commit (89-01), which eats its own dogfood — all 6 P89 rows carry the field; verifier ships in 89-07.

asserts: load_catalog() refuses any row minted on/after 2026-05-08 (minted_at >= cutoff when present, else last_verified >= cutoff) that is missing a claim_vs_assertion_audit string >=50 chars

load_catalog() GRANDFATHERS a row present at the RBF-FW-11-landing commit (8413184, GRANDFATHERED_NULL_LV) even when its last_verified is null and it lacks the field — a null last_verified is NOT treated as 'new' (catalog_dirty() drops it from unchanged rows), the grandfather rule keys off landing-commit presence not the null proxy

load_catalog() STILL refuses a null-last_verified row that is NOT in the landing grandfather set and lacks the field — closing the 'omit last_verified to dodge the audit requirement' hole a bare null->grandfathered heuristic would open
load_catalog() additionally refuses any row with kind: shell-subprocess that lacks expected.artifact.transcript_path (or equivalent transcript-emitting contract)
SystemExit message names the catalog path + row id + cutoff date, and teaches the grandfather distinction

rows.owner_hint: add a claim_vs_assertion_audit paragraph (>=50 chars) to the row explaining how the verifier would falsify the description claim if false; for kind:shell-subprocess rows ALSO declare a transcript path. A null-last_verified NEW row (not present at landing commit 8413184) is NOT grandfathered — it must carry the field.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier asserts that `python3 -m unittest quality.runners.test_audit_field` (a) rejects a synthetic row dated 2026-05-08 with no `claim_vs_assertion_audit` via `SystemExit(FAIL: ...missing claim_vs_assertion_audit (>=50 chars required...))`, (b) GRANDFATHERS a row whose fully-qualified key is in GRANDFATHERED_NULL_LV (present at the RBF-FW-11-landing commit 8413184) with last_verified=null and no field — load_catalog() succeeds, (c) STILL rejects a null-last_verified row NOT in that set and lacking the field (the dodge-close), and (d) rejects any `kind: shell-subprocess` row lacking an emitting-transcript contract. If the description's claim ('the runner enforces the field on rows minted >= 2026-05-08, grandfathers rows present at the landing commit — keyed off commit presence, NOT a null-last_verified proxy — and still forces the field on genuinely-new null-last_verified rows, AND enforces transcript_path on kind:shell-subprocess rows') were false — e.g., the grandfather rule keyed off null last_verified (mis-grandfathering new rows and re-opening the dodge), or force-classified the 4 legacy null-lv rows as new, or the transcript sub-rule was missing — the assertion would fail because the synthetic grandfathered catalog would raise, or the synthetic new null-lv catalog would load, and the test would error.

rows.comment: Stage-2 catalog-row minting (2026-07-04). Prerequisite doc fixes (eager-window ledger items) landed in the same phase: docs/reference/cli.md gained attach + sync sections, docs/reference/exit-codes.md gained attach + sync rows plus re-derived (fn-name, not line-number) citations, docs/reference/crates.md fixed 'nine crates' -> ten + added a reposix-quality section.

asserts: every variant of the clap Cmd enum in crates/reposix-cli/src/main.rs (except Version, which has no dedicated cli.md prose section by long-standing convention) has a matching '## `reposix <name>`' section in docs/reference/cli.md
every variant (including Version) has a row in docs/reference/exit-codes.md's per-subcommand table

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a new/renamed Cmd enum variant in crates/reposix-cli/src/main.rs has no matching section in docs/reference/cli.md or no row in docs/reference/exit-codes.md -- add both in the same PR
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier regex-parses `enum Cmd { ... }` in main.rs for variant names, then for each non-exempt variant greps docs/reference/cli.md for a `## `reposix <name>`` heading (kebab-cased) and for every variant greps docs/reference/exit-codes.md for a backtick-wrapped row naming that subcommand. If the claim ('every CLI subcommand is documented in both reference pages') were false -- e.g. a new `reposix attach` variant shipped with no cli.md section -- the heading regex would find zero matches for 'attach' and the verifier would exit 1 naming the undocumented variant.

rows.comment: D-CONV-3 (2026-07-04, quality/SURPRISES.md 'Quality Convergence'): the scripts/ collapse deleted 6 dead shims + ~15 one-shot phase scripts, collapsed a duplicate validator pair, and converted quality/catalogs/orphan-scripts.json to the cadences-list schema. This row is the INVERSE gate that keeps the collapse from silently regressing -- the existing orphan-scripts-audit.py --all mode only re-checks rows that already exist, so it is structurally blind to a brand-new file dropped in scripts/ with no registry row.

asserts: every file directly under scripts/ (excluding the scripts/migrations/ subdirectory, exempt by policy) has EITHER a matching row in quality/catalogs/orphan-scripts.json OR is referenced by a live-wiring surface (.githooks/, .github/workflows/, docs/, CLAUDE.md, quality/gates/, quality/catalogs/, quality/PROTOCOL.md, or .claude/skills/)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a new file landed in scripts/ (excl. migrations/) with no orphan-scripts.json row and no reference from any live-wiring surface -- either add a registry row (mirroring an existing one) or wire it into a hook/workflow/doc and let this gate's grep pick it up.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If a new orphan script were added to scripts/ without a registry row or any live-wiring reference, the inverse scan's per-file loop would find it absent from both the `registered` id-set (derived from orphan-scripts.json rows) and every haystack file's text (grepped for `scripts/<basename>`), append it to `orphans`, and flip the row to FAIL naming the exact orphaned path -- the completeness claim is falsified the moment a single untracked file exists.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-06 (P90 90-02) — transport/perf-claim rows must carry coverage_kind: real-backend or an explicit WAIVED+until_date; hard-enforced at catalog-load time for rows minted >= P90 (D90-05), RAISE-only for legacy P78–P88 rows until P95 RBF-D-06 migrates them. transport_claim is a tri-state: explicit true/false wins, absent falls back to a regex over comment+id for dims perf/agent-ux/security (P90-D1).

asserts: is_transport_or_perf_row honors transport_claim tri-state (true→transport, false→suppress, absent→regex over comment+id in dims perf/agent-ux/security)
a transport-detected row minted >= P90 lacking coverage_kind AND lacking a compliant waiver → SystemExit at load
a legacy (pre-cutoff) transport row lacking coverage_kind loads clean (RAISE-only)
non-transport mechanical row without coverage_kind loads clean (no over-fire)

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/_audit_field.py gains is_transport_or_perf_row() + the coverage_kind hard-check; verifier ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs python3 -m unittest quality.runners.test_audit_field.TestCoverageKind, which constructs synthetic rows exercising all three transport_claim states plus the minted_at cutoff split. If the description's claim ("a new transport/perf row minted at P90 or later without coverage_kind or a compliant waiver is rejected at load, while legacy pre-cutoff rows only RAISE") were false — e.g. the hard/RAISE split were inverted, or the transport_claim tri-state collapsed to a boolean and ignored the explicit-false override — the synthetic new-row case would load silently (no SystemExit) or the legacy-row case would wrongly hard-block, and the corresponding assertion would fail.

rows.comment: D90-03 (cross-AI H2 drain) — minted_at becomes the write-once audit-cutoff anchor for claim_vs_assertion_audit enforcement: when present it is the sole anchor (immutable), and the validator anchors on it in preference to the legacy last_verified heuristic. A row minted >= P90_MINT_CUTOFF (2026-07-05Z) without minted_at is rejected only via the existing last_verified>=cutoff guard; null-last_verified legacy rows are not retroactively forced (P90-D2 residual, drains at P95 RBF-D-06).

asserts: when minted_at present it is the sole audit-cutoff anchor (immutable; a hand-edited last_verified no longer moves the cutoff)
a row with minted_at >= P90_MINT_CUTOFF (2026-07-05Z) and no minted_at is rejected only via the lv>=cutoff guard (null-lv legacy rows NOT forced)
full-catalog load sweep succeeds at landing (no existing row trips the reject)

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/_audit_field.py: anchor claim_vs_assertion_audit cutoff on minted_at when present; verifier ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs python3 -m unittest quality.runners.test_audit_field.TestMintedAt, which includes a backdate-attack fixture: a row carrying minted_at=2026-06-... alongside a hand-edited last_verified=2026-04-... (an attempt to dodge the cutoff by rewriting the older, no-longer-authoritative field). If the description's claim ("minted_at, once present, is the sole immutable audit-cutoff anchor") were false — e.g. the validator still consulted last_verified when minted_at was present — that backdate fixture would load without the claim_vs_assertion_audit requirement being enforced, and the test asserting rejection would fail. A second fixture asserts the full live catalog sweep still loads clean at landing time, guarding against an over-eager anchor swap breaking existing rows.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-07a (cross-AI H4 drain) — a row whose verifier.script path does not exist on disk at grade time must flip to NOT-VERIFIED, never silently preserve a stale PASS. The artifact distinguishes a deploy/path glitch (error: verifier not found at <path>) from a real regression FAIL so on-call triage doesn't confuse the two. The transient _verifier_missing marker is stripped before the artifact is persisted.

asserts: a PASS row whose verifier.script is absent flips to NOT-VERIFIED (never preserves PASS)
the artifact carries error='verifier not found at <path>' distinguishing a deploy glitch from a real FAIL
_verifier_missing transient flag is stripped before persist

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/run.py: missing-verifier-script branch demotes to NOT-VERIFIED with the error marker; ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs a new test class asserting that a synthetic catalog row with status: PASS and a verifier.script path pointed at a nonexistent file, when re-graded, ends up with status NOT-VERIFIED and an artifact containing error='verifier not found at <path>', with no residual _verifier_missing key persisted. If the description's claim ("a missing verifier script demotes PASS to NOT-VERIFIED rather than preserving the stale grade") were false — e.g. today's single preserve-everything branch stayed intact — the fixture's re-grade would leave status: PASS untouched and the assertion would fail.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-07b (cross-AI M8, AMENDED D90-04 per D90-12 item 2) — env-gated skip of pre-release-real-backend rows (creds/allowlist missing) is fail-closed WITH preserved history: current status flips and PERSISTS NOT-VERIFIED for ALL such rows including the P0 litmus (never a skip-as-pass channel, OD-2-safe), while the prior REAL grade survives in last_real_grade + last_real_verified write-history fields (written only when the prior status was PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL), and the artifact/report carries skip_reason: env-missing so the churn is explained. A second cred-less run is idempotent (byte-identical catalog).

asserts: an env-gated skip flips and persists status NOT-VERIFIED for ALL pre-release-real-backend rows including the P0 litmus (fail-closed, OD-2-safe)
the prior real grade is preserved in last_real_grade + last_real_verified write-history fields (written only when prior status was PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL)
the skip artifact/report carries skip_reason: env-missing so the churn is explained
a second cred-less run after the flip leaves the catalog byte-identical (idempotent)

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/_realbackend.py: env-gate skip branch gains fail-closed-with-history semantics; ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: MUST match the fail-closed-with-history contract (NOT the rejected preserve-PASS wording). The verifier runs quality.runners.test_realbackend.TestRunRowEnvGateIntegration, which fixtures a row previously graded PASS by a real run, then re-runs it with REPOSIX_ALLOWED_ORIGINS and creds scrubbed from the environment. If the description's claim ("a cred-less run can never ride a stale real PASS; history + skip_reason explain the demotion") were false, the test would observe one of two failure shapes: status still PASS (the skip-as-pass channel OD-2 forbids reopening), or status NOT-VERIFIED with no last_real_grade/skip_reason recorded (silent ground-truth loss — M8's actual complaint). Either observation fails the assertion; only the fail-closed-with-history shape passes.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-08 (cross-AI M6) — kind:shell-subprocess rows declare a transcript-emitting contract at load time (RBF-FW-11 precedent), but nothing today enforces the contract was actually FULFILLED at grade time. This row closes that gap: a kind:shell-subprocess row that computed PASS on verifier exit 0 is downgraded to FAIL at grade time unless artifact.transcript_path names a file that actually exists and contains an argv: line, proving a real subprocess transcript was written, not merely promised in the catalog row.

asserts: a kind:shell-subprocess row computing PASS on exit 0 is downgraded to FAIL unless artifact.transcript_path names an existing file containing an argv: line
non-shell-subprocess rows are unaffected (no over-fire)
the live agent-ux/kind-shell-subprocess-worked-example row still PASSes (its verifier emits a real transcript)

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/run.py: _run_synthetic gains a post-exit-0 transcript-existence check for kind:shell-subprocess rows; ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier extends quality.runners.test_realbackend._run_synthetic with a kind:shell-subprocess fixture whose verifier script exits 0 but never writes the transcript file it names. If the description's claim ("a shell-subprocess row cannot PASS on exit-0 alone; the transcript must actually exist with an argv: line") were false — e.g. the runner trusted exit code alone, as it does today — that fixture would grade PASS despite the missing transcript, and the assertion would fail. A companion fixture asserts non-shell-subprocess rows are unaffected, and a live-catalog fixture asserts the real agent-ux/kind-shell-subprocess-worked-example row still PASSes because its verifier genuinely emits a transcript.

rows.comment: F-K4b / ROADMAP SC2 (confirmed shipping per D90-12 item 1: per-expected-assert design ruled by D90-12 item 1, defer-to-P95 rejected) — today's grading only checks that verifier-written asserts_passed and a row's expected.asserts share SOME vocabulary overlap globally, which let the p86 F6 shape (9 expected vs 17 passed, heavy shared vocabulary, 2 expected uncovered) grade PASS dishonestly. This row requires per-expected-assert coverage: when both lists are non-empty, EVERY expected.asserts entry must map to at least one asserts_passed entry via normalized per-pair token matching; any unmatched expected assert blocks the PASS flip and asserts_failed names the unmatched entries. The check is a no-op when asserts_passed is empty (backward-compat with the ~91 pre-existing mechanical rows that don't yet emit per-assert results).

asserts: when BOTH expected.asserts and verifier-written asserts_passed are non-empty, EVERY expected.asserts entry must map to >=1 asserts_passed entry via normalized per-pair token matching; any unmatched expected assert blocks the PASS flip and asserts_failed names the unmatched entries
when asserts_passed is empty (verifier wrote none) the check is a no-op (backward-compat with the 91 mechanical rows)
the p86 F6 shape (9 expected vs 17 passed, heavy shared vocabulary, 2 expected uncovered) goes RED
zero false RED across all existing catalog rows against their current artifacts

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/run.py: grade-time per-expected-assert congruence check; ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs the asserts-congruence cases in quality.runners.test_audit_field (congruence helper at quality/runners/_audit_field.py; p86 F6 fixture at test_audit_field.py — 9 expected.asserts vs 17 asserts_passed, heavy shared vocabulary, 2 of the 9 expected entries genuinely uncovered by any passed entry). If the description's claim ("every expected assert must be covered by at least one passed assert via per-pair matching, not merely global vocabulary overlap") were false — e.g. only a global bag-of-tokens overlap check ran — the F6 fixture would grade PASS (the shared vocabulary masks the 2 uncovered entries) and the assertion expecting RED would fail. Zero-false-RED on the live catalog was verified by the 90-02 landing sweep and the honestly-green pre-push sweep at 76ef11b (49 PASS, 0 FAIL), not by a dedicated fixture; congruence is gated on minted_at (new-regime rows) per D90-05.

rows.comment: P94 D4 — catalog-freshness sweep before v0.13.0 milestone-close. Re-grade every STALE (TTL-expired or session-stale) NON-P93 catalog row across all dimensions and confirm no NEW code regression hides behind a stale NOT-VERIFIED/FAIL. PRE-ESTABLISHED FACT baked into the GREEN contract (do NOT re-triage as a bug): the agent-ux/p92-mid-stream-litmus-t1-t4 FAIL is the EXPECTED git<2.34 environment gate — local exit 75 on the dev box's git 2.25.1; the same row PASSED on CI's git 2.54.0. It is an accounted-for env-gate, NOT a code regression; any similar exit-75 env-gate FAIL is likewise accounted-for. GREEN = every stale non-P93 row re-graded AND every resulting FAIL is a NAMED, accounted-for env-gate (not a genuine code regression). Milestone-close readiness gate; subagent adjudicates 'env-gate vs regression' from the runner output.
sources: quality/runners/run.py (the sweep engine — re-grades stale rows)
quality/catalogs/agent-ux.json (agent-ux/p92-mid-stream-litmus-t1-t4 — the accounted-for git<2.34 env-gate FAIL)
rows.command: bash .planning/phases/94-real-backend-frictions/94-D4-sweep.sh && then revert catalog self-mutation: git checkout HEAD -- quality/catalogs/   (run.py has NO --all flag; the driver runs every cadence once = canonical all-rows sweep, per GOOD-TO-HAVES-03)

asserts: every STALE (session-stale or freshness_ttl-expired) NON-P93 catalog row is re-graded in the sweep (no stale row left un-run)
every resulting FAIL is a NAMED, accounted-for environment gate (e.g. agent-ux/p92-mid-stream-litmus-t1-t4 exit 75 on git<2.34, PASSED on CI git 2.54.0) — NOT a genuine code regression
the accounted-for env-gates are enumerated with their exit code + the reason they are not regressions (subagent-graded adjudication, not a bare pass)
no re-graded row flips PASS->FAIL for a reason attributable to a code change in this milestone (a true regression BLOCKS the milestone-close verdict)

rows.owner_hint: NOT RUN this session (catalog-first SPEC mint). Run LAST in P94 (after D1-D3 land): `python3 quality/runners/run.py --all` then inspect the verdict for stale-row re-grades. For each FAIL, classify env-gate (exit 75 / creds-absent / git<2.34) vs code-regression; a subagent adjudicates and records the enumeration. The p92-mid-stream git<2.34 exit-75 FAIL is pre-accounted (reproduce on git 2.54 container to confirm it PASSES there, per 92/93 REPRO-NOTES). Any UN-accounted FAIL BLOCKS milestone-close.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier re-grades stale non-P93 rows and the subagent enumerates every FAIL as env-gate-or-regression. If the description's claim ('the milestone can close because every stale row was re-graded and every FAIL is an accounted-for env-gate, none a code regression') were false — e.g. a stale row was skipped (never re-graded), or a genuine PASS->FAIL code regression was silently bucketed as an 'env-gate', or the p92-mid-stream FAIL was passed off without confirming it PASSES on git 2.54 — the per-FAIL classification would name an un-accounted regression and the assert requiring zero code-regressions would fail. Milestone-close readiness gate (structure dimension); 30d TTL so it must be re-run fresh at the actual close.

rows.comment: D-P96-01 (.planning/CONSULT-DECISIONS.md) — the quality runner splits GRADE from PERSIST: a bare cadence run (the pre-push / pre-pr GATE path) grades in memory + writes per-row artifacts + still blocks RED, but does NOT write status back to quality/catalogs/. Only run.py --cadence <c> --persist (the phase-close / verifier-subagent MINT path) mutates the committed catalog. Closes the HIGH self-mutation bug where a read-only pre-push flipped docs-build.json (NOT-VERIFIED->PASS) and dirtied the tree at push time (3 live repros, each dropped with the recurring `git checkout HEAD -- quality/catalogs/` band-aid, now unnecessary).

asserts: a bare cadence run (run.py --cadence pre-push, no --persist) leaves every quality/catalogs/*.json byte-identical (validate-only, no self-mutation)
a validate-only cadence run still blocks RED via compute_exit_code exit 1 (gate integrity preserved without persistence)
the explicit mint path (run.py --cadence <c> --persist) still writes graded status back to the catalog (grades not frozen)

rows.owner_hint: If RED: a run.py cadence run is writing catalog status as a side effect. Do NOT re-add `git checkout HEAD -- quality/catalogs/`. Fix the split in run.py:main() — save_catalog must be gated behind args.persist (default False), and minting must use `run.py --cadence <c> --persist`. Hermetic repro: quality/runners/test_run.py.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier (A) drives run.main() over a synthetic would-flip catalog and asserts a no-persist run leaves it byte-identical, that a validate-only RED still exits 1, and that --persist still writes the flip; and (B) snapshots md5 of every real quality/catalogs/*.json, runs a real cadence, and asserts zero bytes changed. If the description's claim ('cadence gate runs never mutate the committed catalog, yet still block RED, and --persist still mints') were false — e.g. run.py:main() called save_catalog on a bare cadence run, the pre-fix bug — layer A would observe the synthetic flip persisted (byte diff) and layer B would observe real-catalog md5 drift, and the verifier would exit 1. Conversely if the fix over-corrected and froze grades, the --persist mint assertion would fail.

rows.comment: P117-06 fix-twice backstop. docs/social/* is `not_in_nav` (mkdocs.yml:56) so mkdocs-strict / link-resolution / doc-alignment / banned-words NEVER scan it — that gap is exactly why a stale current-tense FUSE line survived to launch in the SC5b bug this phase purged (PATTERNS.md NOTICED #3). This is the minimal grep-based backstop closing that gap: scan every docs/social/**/*.md for known-dead architecture terms (FUSE, /mnt/, mount) and BLOCK if any survive, with a `<!-- social-freshness: ok — reason -->` allowlist marker for deliberate PAST-tense history (mirrors docs/development/roadmap.md's own historical framing of the same v0.4.0 FUSE-mount milestone, and docs/.banned-words.toml's `<!-- banned-words: ok -->` escape-hatch convention).

asserts: no docs/social/**/*.md contains a known-dead architecture term (FUSE, /mnt/, mount) — case-insensitive, word-boundary anchored to avoid false hits on amount/paramount (T-117-08)
on a match, exit 1 names the offending file:line plus a fix (reword, or the allowlist-marker escape hatch for deliberate history)
a line carrying the `social-freshness: ok` allowlist marker is skipped even if it contains a dead term

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/social/**/*.md picked up a current-tense FUSE/mount/`/mnt/` reference — since docs/social/* is not_in_nav, no other docs gate would have caught it. Reword to the git-native partial-clone model, or if it's deliberate past-tense history (matching roadmap.md's framing) append the `<!-- social-freshness: ok — reason -->` marker to the line.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If a stale current-tense FUSE/mount claim reached docs/social/**/*.md (the exact SC5b bug class), `bash quality/gates/structure/social-freshness.sh` would grep-match `\bFUSE\b`, `/mnt/`, or `\bmount\b` on that line and exit 1, naming the file:line and a fix — the selftest (social-freshness.selftest.sh) proves this BLOCK path with a planted FUSE/`.mnt/`/mount line each independently, proves the word-boundary anchor holds against amount/paramount (T-117-08's named false-positive risk), and proves the allowlist-marker escape hatch un-blocks a marked line. If the gate's claim were false — e.g. a regex typo silently matching nothing — the selftest's block-path assertions (b)/(c)/(d) would observe exit 0 instead of 1 and fail.

rows.comment: P123/SC1 (DRAIN-03) — closes the false-green-preflight / silent-skip gap (.planning/milestones/v0.15.0-phases/SURPRISES-INTAKE.md 2026-07-14 20:43 HIGH entry): quality/runners/run.py does not source ./.env while scripts/preflight-real-backends.sh does, so the pre-release-real-backend cadence silently skips every real-backend row to NOT-VERIFIED when invoked without the caller pre-sourcing .env, while preflight independently reports the backends reachable -- the two together give a false impression of full coverage. run.py must conditionally self-source ./.env (present-only, non-clobbering) so the cadence exercises real creds instead of silently skipping.

asserts: a ./.env at repo root is parsed and its KEY=VALUE pairs exported into os.environ, but ONLY for keys not already present (explicit shell env always wins)
no .env present is a silent no-op -- no error, no CI behavior change
no secret VALUE is ever printed to stdout/stderr -- only which KEY names were loaded, if any

rows.owner_hint: if RED/NOT-VERIFIED: mirror scripts/preflight-real-backends.sh's `if [ -f ./.env ] && [ -z "${VAR:-}..." ]; then set -a; . ./.env; set +a; fi` pattern inside run.py's own startup (before catalog load), so `run.py --cadence pre-release-real-backend` behaves identically whether or not the caller pre-sourced .env.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If run.py failed to self-source ./.env, or clobbered an already-exported credential, or printed a secret value, the verifier would drive run.py against a synthetic .env fixture and: (a) assert a shell-exported KEY survives untouched (explicit env wins) -- failing if run.py overwrote it; (b) assert a KEY present ONLY in .env lands in run.py's os.environ -- failing if run.py never sourced the file; (c) assert stdout/stderr contain KEY names but never the VALUE -- failing on any leak; (d) assert a missing .env is a silent no-op -- failing on any error or behavior change. Each predicate independently falsifies the claim if run.py's dotenv-sourcing were absent, wrong-precedence, or leaky.

rows.comment: P123/SC2 (DRAIN-04) — closes the silent-catalog-corruption gap (.planning/milestones/v0.15.0-phases/SURPRISES-INTAKE.md 2026-07-14 20:44 HIGH entry -- a real near-miss: a run downgraded vision-litmus PASS->FAIL in the --persist write, caught only by manual `git restore` before commit). --persist must refuse to write a row from a committed-GREEN status (per `git show HEAD:<catalog>`, meaning PASS/WAIVED) to worse without --allow-downgrade.

asserts: a row whose committed status is PASS or WAIVED, and whose freshly graded status is anything else, is NOT persisted unless --allow-downgrade is passed
the refusal prints the row id, old status, new status, and the literal recovery command `python3 quality/runners/run.py --cadence <c> --persist --allow-downgrade`
a row absent from the git-HEAD committed catalog (brand-new) is exempt and mints freely
--allow-downgrade restores the prior unconditional-persist behavior, still printing a loud per-row notice

rows.owner_hint: if RED: --persist's write path lacks the pre-write committed-status comparison; add a `git show HEAD:<catalog>` read + status-ordinal comparison (PASS/WAIVED outrank everything else) before each row write, refuse without --allow-downgrade, and print the literal recovery command named in expected.asserts.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If --persist silently downgraded a committed PASS/WAIVED row to a worse status without --allow-downgrade, the verifier would seed a synthetic catalog with a committed PASS row, force a fresh grade to FAIL, invoke run.py --persist, and assert the committed file is UNCHANGED (still PASS) -- a silent overwrite would flip that assertion. It further asserts the printed refusal names the row id / old status / new status / the literal --allow-downgrade recovery command -- a missing or vague message fails that assertion. Finally it asserts --allow-downgrade DOES persist the downgrade with a loud notice, and that a brand-new row (absent from git HEAD) mints freely without needing the flag -- proving the guard is scoped to downgrades of PRE-EXISTING green rows only, not a blanket freeze.

rows.comment: P123/SC3 (DRAIN-05, cites GTH-V15-01) — two concurrent --persist runners must not interleave writes to the same catalog file -- an advisory OS-level flock wraps the whole check+write critical section. GTH-V15-01: a live near-miss observed two runners minting the same catalog file mid-verification (P104, PID 351077 held the lock while the verifier ran).

asserts: a second --persist invocation targeting the same catalog while a first holds the lock BLOCKS until release, rather than interleaving writes
the lock is scoped to --persist runs only -- a validate-only run never contends for it
single-writer --persist behavior (mint / no-op / RED, from the existing TestPersistGate suite) is unchanged

rows.owner_hint: if RED: wrap the check+write critical section of --persist in an advisory flock (e.g. Python's fcntl.flock) scoped to a per-catalog lockfile; validate-only runs must not acquire it.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If two concurrent --persist invocations against the same catalog file were allowed to interleave, the verifier would launch two run.py --persist processes concurrently against one throwaway catalog copy (one artificially slowed) and assert the SECOND writer BLOCKS until the first releases rather than both writers racing to a torn/interleaved JSON file -- a missing flock would let the second writer proceed immediately and the resulting file would fail to round-trip through json.load or would drop one writer's row, falsifying the claim. It further asserts a concurrent validate-only (no --persist) run never blocks on the lock, and that the existing TestPersistGate mint/no-op/RED behaviors are unchanged with the lock in place.

rows.comment: P123/SC4 (DRAIN-06, cites GTH-V15-03) — a row backed by a missing or non-executable verifier.script is a framework-integrity hole -- a false PASS could ride on a verifier that can't run. Scans every catalog row (excluding *-allowlist.json and the docs-alignment dimension, which has no per-row verifier.script field) and asserts every declared script exists AND is executable. NOT tagged pre-commit yet -- this row's own verifier script does not exist until 123-06 lands in wave 5; a P1 NOT-VERIFIED row tagged pre-commit here would self-block every commit through wave 5. 123-06 adds pre-commit to this row's cadences once its verifier script exists on disk.

asserts: every row with a non-null verifier.script in every quality/catalogs/*.json (excluding *-allowlist.json and docs-alignment) resolves to a file that exists on disk
every resolved verifier.script carries the executable bit (os.access(path, os.X_OK))
a row whose verifier.script is null/absent (outside docs-alignment) is ALSO a violation
a violation prints the offending catalog + row id + path + a concrete fix (chmod +x, or author/correct the path)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: the printed violation names the offending catalog+row id+path; missing file -> author quality/gates/<dim>/<name>.sh; non-executable -> chmod +x <path>; null/absent verifier.script (outside docs-alignment) -> add the field pointing at a real script.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If any catalog row declared a verifier.script that does not exist on disk, or exists but lacks the executable bit, or omitted verifier.script entirely (outside docs-alignment, which has no such field), the verifier would iterate every quality/catalogs/*.json (excluding *-allowlist.json and doc-alignment.json), resolve each row's verifier.script path, and assert os.path.isfile() AND os.access(path, os.X_OK) both hold -- a missing file fails isfile(), a non-executable file fails access(), and a null/absent script fails an explicit not-null assertion. Any one of these failure modes would surface as a printed catalog+row id+path+concrete fix (chmod +x or author the path), directly falsifying the claim that every row's verifier is real and runnable.
